task.prompt: When using nested Flask blueprints, the URL prefix composition is incorrect. A parent blueprint registered with url_prefix='/api' contains a child blueprint with url_prefix='/v2'. The child blueprint has a route '/users'. The expected URL is '/api/v2/users', but under certain registration orders — specifically when the child blueprint is registered to the parent before the parent is registered to the app — the parent's prefix is silently dropped, producing '/v2/users'. The issue does not manifest when blueprints are registered in the reverse order. Investigate the blueprint registration lifecycle and fix the prefix composition for nested blueprints.

hint_levels.symptom_only: Nested blueprint URL prefixes are silently dropped depending on registration order. Registering a child blueprint to a parent before the parent is registered to the app causes the parent's url_prefix to be lost in the composed URL.
hint_levels.file_hint: The bug is in src/flask/blueprints.py in the Blueprint.register() method and src/flask/scaffold.py in the _merge_blueprint_state() helper. When a child is registered to a parent that hasn't been registered to an app yet, the deferred registration list doesn't capture the parent's prefix.
hint_levels.function_hint: In src/flask/blueprints.py register() around line 270, the method iterates self._blueprints (children) and calls child.register(app, ...) with the merged options. But the state.url_prefix used in _merge_blueprint_state() at line 310 reads from self.url_prefix which is the creation-time prefix, not the registration-time prefix from the options dict. When register_blueprint() is called on the parent with a different url_prefix, the mismatch causes the parent prefix to be lost for already-queued children.

reasoning_chain: Read the symptom: nested blueprint prefixes dropped when parent isn't registered to app yet
Open src/flask/blueprints.py and find Blueprint.register() — trace how child blueprints are iterated
Find that register() calls child.register(app, options) for each child in self._blueprints
Trace _merge_blueprint_state() in src/flask/scaffold.py — see that it reads self.url_prefix (creation-time)
Realize: when parent is registered with a DIFFERENT url_prefix via app.register_blueprint(parent, url_prefix='/api'), the creation-time prefix on the parent object is '/old', not '/api'
Open src/flask/app.py to see how register_blueprint passes options to blueprint.register()
Open tests/test_blueprints.py to see existing nested blueprint tests — no test for differing creation-time vs registration-time prefixes
Understand the fix: _merge_blueprint_state must use the registration-time prefix from options, not self.url_prefix
Apply fix: pass the effective parent prefix through to child registration, use options['url_prefix'] in _merge_blueprint_state
